global.title: Dispersed replicas are a one-field opt-in via bee-js RedundancyLevel
global.content:
## Dispersed replicas via `RedundancyLevel` — additive, reversible

bee-js exposes:

```ts
enum RedundancyLevel { OFF=0, MEDIUM=1, STRONG=2, INSANE=3, PARANOID=4 }
enum RedundancyStrategy { NONE, DATA, PROX, RACE }
```

Uploading with `{ redundancyLevel: MEDIUM }` makes Bee erasure-code the chunks across the neighbourhood. The default download strategy cascade is `NONE → DATA → PROX → RACE`, so existing readers transparently benefit — no reader-side code changes needed.

This is **not** the paper's literal balanced-SOC-replica pattern (paper §5.1.3) but achieves the same outcome through Reed-Solomon coding, as a one-field opt-in with zero protocol changes.

**Why it's low-risk:**
- No schema changes, no wire-protocol changes, no feed-shape changes.
- ACT layering is unaffected — redundancy operates on chunks, ACT on their contents.
- Rolling back = revert the 2–3 line flag additions.
- Zero behavioural change if `RedundancyLevel.OFF` (default).
global.description: bee-js exposes RedundancyLevel (OFF..PARANOID); upload with redundancyLevel:MEDIUM and Bee erasure-codes chunks. Readers transparently RACE-fetch via the default cascade.
